app.name: The Library
a11y.title: Accessibility
a11y.resetDefaultsTitle: Reset all accessibility settings to defaults
account.title: My Account
library.title: The Library
library.subtitle: A Private Collection of Audiobooks
library.noResults: No audiobooks found matching your search.
book.downloadTooltip: Download this audiobook for offline listening in a local player. The Library streams from its own server storage and cannot access files on your device.
library.signInDesc: Playing and downloading audiobooks is available to members.
library.errorLoading: Error loading your library. Please try again.
library.errorGrouped: Error loading grouped view. Please ensure the API server is running.
library.emptyLibrary: Your library is empty. Start listening to build your collection!
duplicates.title: Duplicates
duplicates.noTitleDuplicates: No audiobooks with matching title and author found.
duplicates.hashHelp: Make sure hashes have been generated first:
duplicates.fileProtected: This file is protected - it is the preferred copy
duplicates.confirmDeleteMsg: You are about to permanently delete {n} audiobook file(s).
duplicates.confirmProtection: The system will automatically protect the last copy of each audiobook to prevent data loss.
login.title: Sign In
login.usernameHelp.title: What is this?
login.usernameHelp.body: This is the name you chose when you signed up. It's like your nickname for this website.
login.usernameHelp.whatTypeBody: Type the exact username you picked when you created your account. It's case-sensitive (capital letters matter).
login.codeHint: Open the app on your phone, find The Library, and type the 6-digit number you see
register.title: Request Access
register.description: Access to The Library is by invitation only. Enter your desired username to request access. An administrator will review your request.
help.title: User Guide
help.comingSoon: Coming soon to your local branch library
help.suggestionHeader: The Librarian Shall Cherish Your Suggestions & Comments
help.thanks: Your words have been received with gratitude.
about.title: About The Library
about.subtitle: A Private Audiobook Collection
contact.title: Contact the Administrator
error.networkError: Network error. Please check your connection.
error.sessionExpired: Your session has expired. Please sign in again.
error.authRequiredDesc: You must sign in to access The Library. Please enter your credentials to continue.
error.forbiddenDesc: You do not have permission to access this resource. If you believe this is an error, please contact the administrator.
notification.passkeyPrompt: Your browser supports passkeys. Setting up a passkey means you can sign in instantly without waiting for an email link. You can switch in your profile settings.
marquee.dismissTitle: Pull the switch to dismiss new books
maintenance.dismissTitle: Dismiss maintenance announcements for this session
book.noSupplements: No supplements available for this audiobook.
subtitleGen.offer: Translated subtitles aren't available for this book yet. Generate them now?
subtitleGen.phase.gpu_spinup: Waking up the GPU server (cold start can take a minute or two)…
subtitleGen.phase.transcribing: Transcribing audio — this can take several minutes…
subtitleGen.phase.error: Subtitle generation failed. Please try again in a few minutes.
verify.subtitle: Checking Your Link
verify.checking: Checking your link... just a moment.
verify.successMessage: Taking you to The Library now...
verify.errorDefault: This can happen if the link is more than 15 minutes old, you already used this link, or the link was copied incorrectly.
verify.resendPrompt: Enter your username below and we'll send you a fresh link.
verify.manualDescription: If you received a code instead of a clickable link, paste it here.
verify.help.whereIsCodeBody: Check your email for a message from The Library. Copy the code from that email and paste it here.
verify.help.checkSpam: Check your spam or junk folder
verify.help.waitAndCheck: Wait a few minutes and check again
verify.js.firewallBlocked: Request was blocked by the firewall. Please try again or contact admin.
verify.js.activated: Your account is activated. Taking you to The Library now...
verify.js.noServer: We couldn't reach the server. Please check your internet connection and try again.
verify.js.newLinkSent: Check your email! We sent you a new sign-in link. Click it to sign in.
verify.js.sendFailed: Unable to send a new link. Try signing in from the login page.
claim.subtitle: Claim Your Credentials
claim.welcome: Welcome! Let's get you set up.
claim.usernameHint: The name that was chosen for you
claim.help.usernameBody: This is the name that was set up for you. Check your invitation email — your username is listed there.
claim.tokenHint: The special code from your invitation email — four groups of letters and numbers
claim.help.tokenWhatBody: This is a special code from your invitation email. It looks like four groups of letters and numbers separated by dashes:
claim.help.tokenWhere1: In the invitation email you received (look for a gold box)
claim.help.tokenWhere2: Written down where you saved it
claim.help.tokenMissingBody: Check your email inbox (and spam folder) for a message from The Library. The token is in a gold box in that email. If you still can't find it, contact the person who invited you.
claim.noTokenCallout: Don't have your token? Check the email you received — the token is in a gold box inside that email. Also check your spam folder.
claim.method.title: How Would You Like to Sign In?
claim.method.welcomeSuffix: ! Pick how you want to prove it's you when you sign in.
claim.method.unsupported: Your browser or device doesn't support this option. Please choose another way to sign in.
claim.method.notSureBody: If you have a modern phone or computer, Use Your Face, Fingerprint, or PIN is the easiest choice. If you just want an email link each time, choose Sign In via Email.
claim.method.comparePasskey: Face / Fingerprint / PIN: Easiest daily use. Sign in the way you unlock your phone. Best for most people.
claim.method.compareFido2: Physical Key: A small device you plug in or tap. Best if you already own one (like a YubiKey).
claim.method.compareTotp: Free Phone App: Type a 6-digit number from your phone each time. Works everywhere.
claim.method.compareMagicLink: Email Link: We email you a sign-in link each time. Simplest option — nothing to install or remember.
claim.method.changeMindTitle: Can I change my mind later?
claim.method.changeMindBody: Yes — you can change your sign-in method anytime in your profile settings.
claim.passkey.title: Use Your Face, Fingerprint, or PIN
claim.passkey.description: Sign in the same way you unlock your phone or computer. Nothing extra to install.
claim.passkey.pro3: Built into iPhones, Androids, Macs, and Windows PCs
claim.passkey.buttonTitle: Set up face, fingerprint, or PIN sign-in
claim.passkey.help.howBody: Your phone or computer already knows how to recognize you — with your face, fingerprint, or a PIN you type. This option uses that same recognition to sign you in. No extra app needed.
claim.passkey.help.step1: You set it up once (right now)
claim.passkey.help.step2: Next time you sign in, your device asks you to unlock it (face, fingerprint, or PIN)
claim.passkey.help.good1: Only works on the device where you set it up
claim.passkey.help.good2: If you lose your device, you can use emergency backup codes
claim.passkey.help.bestForBody: Most people! Especially if you'll use the same phone or computer each time.
claim.fido2.title: Use a Physical Key
claim.fido2.description: A small device you plug in or tap against your phone. You would need to buy one ($25–50).
claim.fido2.pro1: Works on any device — just bring your key
claim.fido2.help.whatBody: It's a small device (like a USB stick or keychain fob) that you plug into your computer or tap against your phone. Popular brands: YubiKey, Google Titan.
claim.fido2.help.step1: You plug in or tap your key
claim.fido2.help.step2: Touch the key when it blinks
claim.fido2.help.good2: You must carry it with you
claim.fido2.help.good3: Works on any device — just bring your key
claim.fido2.help.bestForBody: People who already own a YubiKey or similar device.
claim.totp.title: Use a Free Phone App
claim.totp.description: Install a free app on your phone that shows you a 6-digit number. Type that number to sign in.
claim.totp.pro1: Works everywhere — any device, any browser
claim.totp.pro2: Free apps available for all phones
claim.totp.pro3: No internet needed on your phone
claim.totp.help.howBody: You install a free app on your phone. The app shows a 6-digit number that changes every 30 seconds. When you sign in, you open the app and type that number.
claim.totp.help.step1: Download a free app (we'll help you pick one)
claim.totp.help.step2: Point your phone's camera at a special code on screen
claim.totp.help.step3: Each time you sign in, open the app and type the 6 digits you see
claim.totp.help.good1: You need your phone nearby each time you sign in
claim.totp.help.good2: You'll need to type 6 digits each time
claim.totp.help.good3: Works on any computer or device — very flexible
claim.totp.help.bestForBody: People who sign in from many different devices, or who want the most flexible option.
claim.totp.welcomeSuffix: ! Follow these steps to finish setting up your account.
claim.totp.printHint: You might want to print this page or write down these steps before continuing.
claim.totp.step1Title: Install a Free App on Your Phone
claim.totp.appHelp.whatBody: It's a free app that shows a 6-digit number that changes every 30 seconds. You'll type this number each time you sign in. That's it!
claim.totp.appHelp.whichBody: is our top pick — free, open source, works on iPhone, Android, and as a browser extension. No account or phone number needed.
claim.totp.installIntro: If you don't already have one of these apps, install one now. They're all free and none of them need your phone number:
claim.totp.app.noAccount: No account or phone number needed.
claim.totp.app.noAccountOpen: No account or phone number needed. Open source and privacy-focused.
claim.totp.step2Title: Scan the Code Below with Your App
claim.totp.qrHelp.title: How to scan
claim.totp.qrHelp.step2: Look for a + button or Add Account
claim.totp.qrHelp.step3: Choose Scan QR Code or Scan Barcode
claim.totp.qrHelp.step4: Point your phone's camera at the black-and-white square below
claim.totp.qrHelp.step5: The app will automatically add "The Library"
claim.totp.scanInstructions: Open your authenticator app. Look for a + button or Add Account. Choose Scan QR Code or Scan Barcode. Then point your phone's camera at the black-and-white square below:
claim.totp.cantScanBody: That's okay! In your app, choose Enter Key Manually instead. Then carefully type the secret code shown in the gold box below.
claim.totp.secretLabel: Secret code (type this into your app if you can't scan):
claim.totp.firstCodePrefix: Almost done! Open your authenticator app and find
claim.totp.firstCodeSuffix: . You'll see a 6-digit number that changes every 30 seconds. Type that number below.
claim.totp.firstCodeNote: Don't worry if the number changes while you're typing — just wait for the new one and type that instead.
claim.magicLink.title: Sign In via Email
claim.magicLink.description: Each time you sign in, we send a link to your email. Click the link and you're in — no codes or apps needed.
claim.magicLink.pro2: Works on any device with email access
claim.magicLink.pro3: No codes to type or apps to set up
claim.magicLink.help.howBody: When you want to sign in, you enter your username and we send a link to your email. Click the link and you're logged in — no codes, no apps, no passwords.
claim.magicLink.help.step1: Enter your username on the login page
claim.magicLink.help.step2: Check your email for a sign-in link
claim.magicLink.help.good1: You need access to your email each time you sign in
claim.magicLink.help.good2: Links expire after 15 minutes for security
claim.magicLink.help.bestForBody: People who want the simplest possible sign-in and always have email access.
claim.recovery.title: Add a Safety Net Email
claim.recovery.description: If you ever get locked out, we can email you a special link to get back in.
claim.recovery.recommend: We strongly recommend adding your email. It's the easiest way to get back in if something goes wrong.
claim.recovery.emailHint: We'll only use this if you get locked out
claim.recovery.help.whyBody: If you ever lose your phone or can't sign in, we'll send a link to this email that lets you get back in. Think of it as a spare key.
claim.recovery.help.skipBody: You'll still get emergency backup codes in the next step. But having an email is much easier to use if something goes wrong.
claim.recovery.skip: Skip this step (you can still use backup codes if locked out)
claim.backup.title: Save Your Emergency Backup Codes
claim.backup.help.whatBodyTotp: Emergency codes that let you sign in if you ever lose your phone or can't open your app. Think of them as spare keys.
claim.backup.help.whatBodyDevice: Emergency codes that let you sign in if you ever lose your device. Think of them as spare keys.
claim.backup.help.whatBodyEmail: Emergency codes that let you sign in if you ever can't access your email. Think of them as spare keys.
claim.backup.help.save1Rest: and keep it with your important documents
claim.backup.introTotp: These are your emergency codes. If you ever lose your phone or can't open your app, type one of these codes to get back in.
claim.backup.introDevice: These are your emergency codes. If you ever lose your device, type one of these codes to get back in.
claim.backup.introEmail: These are your emergency codes. If you ever can't access your email, type one of these codes to get back in.
claim.backup.keepSafeBold: Write these codes down on paper and keep them somewhere safe
claim.backup.keepSafeRest: (like with your important documents). Each code works only once — cross it off after you use it.
claim.backup.keepSafeShortBold: Write these codes down on paper and keep them somewhere safe.
claim.backup.keepSafeShortRest: Each code works only once — cross it off after you use it.
claim.backup.tip: Tip: Print this page, or take a photo with another device.
claim.webauthn.welcomeSuffix: ! Your account is ready. Before you go in, save your emergency backup codes below.
claim.magicLinkSuccess.welcomeMid: ! Your account is ready. To sign in, just click
claim.magicLinkSuccess.welcomeMid2: on the login page and we'll send a link to
claim.js.firewallBlocked: Request was blocked by the firewall. Please try again or contact admin.
claim.js.passkeyMaybe: Your device may not support built-in passkeys, but you can try.
claim.js.enterBoth: Please enter both username and claim token
claim.js.pendingReview: Your request is still pending admin review. Please check back later.
claim.js.emailRequired: Your email is how we'll send you sign-in links. This is required for email link authentication.
claim.js.recoveryOptionalDesc: Add a recovery email in case you lose access to your authenticator.
claim.js.regCancelled: Registration was cancelled or timed out
claim.js.enter6Digit: Please enter the 6-digit code from your authenticator app
claim.js.invalidCode: Invalid code. Check your authenticator app and try again.
